message:
Last winter, my kid asked why we were pulling out the emergency kit. Instead of making it sound serious, I said, 'Let's do a supplies inventory party!' We spread everything out on the kitchen floor, made a snack run to replace what we'd used, and honestly? Turned what could've been a chore into this weirdly cozy afternoon together.

I think that's the secret nobody talks about — prep doesn't have to feel heavy or scary. It can just be... taking care of things, maybe with someone you love. Go-bags, water rotation, keeping copies of important documents somewhere safe — they're all just love in action, really.

Do you have a prep task you've been putting off? What would make it feel a little lighter?
